LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANNIKA BARNES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING, BUT MODIFIES THE TRIAL DATE AS REQUESTED AND AGREED UPON BY COUNSEL. GRANTED AND THE TRIAL IS CONTINUED FROM JUNE 5, 2017, TO JANUARY 8, 2018, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPARTMENT 206, AND THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF DATE IS EXTENDED TO JUNE 30, 2017, TO PERMIT DEFENDANTS TO TAKE DISCOVERY AS TO THE NEW ALLEGATIONS. MS. BARNES HAS SHOWN GOOD CAUSE TO FILE HER PROPOSED AMENDED COMPLAINT AND GRANTING THE MOTION IS STRONGLY FAVORED BY CALIFORNIA'S LIBERAL POLICY OF ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS. WHILE GRANTING THE MOTION MOOTS THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION CURRENTLY SET FOR HEARING ON MAY 3, 2017, DEFENDANTS WILL NOT BE PREJUDICED BY THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION BECAUSE OF THEIR ABILITY TO FILE SUMMARY MOTIONS TO BE HEARD BEFORE THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

The cause is submitted on the tentative ruling without contest by either side as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS RYDER TRUCK RENTAL, INC., RR DONNELLEY & SONS COMPANY's Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Cross Complaint Naming Quality Driver Solutions Inc. As Roe 1 And Adding Causes Of Action For Express Indemnity And Breach Of Contract is GRANTED on certain conditions that must be explicitly accepted by RR Donnelley no later than September 29, 2017 by a writing sent to all other parties. If the conditions are not so accepted, the motion is denied. The conditions are: a) no later than October 6, 2017 RR Donnelly must serve verified code-compliant responses without any objections other than privilege to deemed-served discovery stating all facts that support its new cross-claims and any previously alleged cross-claims now alleged against new cross-defendants, identifying and providing full contact information for all persons who have knowledge of those facts and stating what facts each person knows, and identifying and producing all documents which refer to or reflect those facts; b) RR Donnelly must agree that cross-defendant Quality Driver Solutions, Inc. may file and serve a summary judgment or adjudication motion on thirty days' notice; and 3) discovery is reopened till thirty days before trial for Quality Driver Solutions to propound any discovery that is related to any of the cross-claims alleged by RR Donnelley. Granting the motion is supported by California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. The conditions imposed on granting the motion eliminate any prejudice to Quality Driver Solutions. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-24-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Amber D. Esposito, of Marcereau & Nazif, Tel. 949.531.6500, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Laurie Book, of Clinton & Clinton, Tel. 562.216.5000, defendants RR Donnelley and Sons Company & Ryder Truck Rental, appearing via CourtCall;
Krista L. DiMercurio, of Magarian & DiMercurio, APLC, Tel. 714.415.3412, for defendants Quality Driver Solutions & David Herrera, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling, but modifies the trial date as requested and agreed upon by counsel. PLAINTIFF ANNIKA BARNES' Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint is granted and the trial is continued from June 5, 2017, to January 8, 2018, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 206, and the discovery cutoff date is extended to June 30, 2017, to permit defendants to take discovery as to the new allegations. Ms. Barnes has shown good cause to file her proposed amended complaint and granting the motion is strongly favored by California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings. While granting the motion moots the summary judgment motion currently set for hearing on May 3, 2017, defendants will not be prejudiced by the granting of this motion because of their ability to file summary motions to be heard before the new trial date. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
